example.target: Pois é, tens razão, devíamos ter saído de casa mais cedo.
example.gloss: That's right, you're right, we should have left home earlier.

example.target: Ora bem, vamos começar a reunião pelo primeiro ponto da agenda.
example.gloss: Well now, let's start the meeting with the first item on the agenda.

words.en: that works out well; good timing

example.target: Calha bem vires hoje, porque preciso da tua ajuda com a mudança.
example.gloss: It works out well that you're coming today, because I need your help with the move.

example.target: Isso não tem nada a ver com o que estávamos a discutir.
example.gloss: That has nothing to do with what we were discussing.

words.en: it doesn't matter; no harm done

example.target: Não faz mal chegares um pouco atrasado, ainda não começámos.
example.gloss: It doesn't matter if you arrive a little late, we haven't started yet.

example.target: Deixa lá, não vale a pena ficares chateado com isso.
example.gloss: Never mind, it's not worth getting upset about that.

example.target: Dá jeito morar perto do metro quando se trabalha no centro.
example.gloss: It's handy to live near the metro when you work in the centre.

words.en: to come in handy, to suit

example.target: Fazia-me jeito que me emprestasses o carro amanhã de manhã.
example.gloss: It would suit me if you lent me the car tomorrow morning.

words.en: to get by, to improvise one's way out of a fix

example.target: Não falava bem inglês, mas desenrascou-se muito bem na entrevista.
example.gloss: He didn't speak English well, but he managed just fine in the interview.

words.en: knack for improvising a quick solution
words.note: informal; often cited as a national trait

example.target: O desenrascanço é visto por muitos como um talento tipicamente português.
example.gloss: Improvised problem-solving is seen by many as a typically Portuguese talent.

example.target: O concerto de ontem foi mesmo fixe, devias ter vindo.
example.gloss: Yesterday's concert was really cool, you should have come.

words.note: very informal youth slang; avoid in writing

example.target: O exame foi bué difícil, quase ninguém acabou a tempo.
example.gloss: The exam was really hard, almost nobody finished in time.

words.note: favourite European intensifier: gostei imenso, custou imenso

example.target: Gostei imenso do jantar, temos de repetir em breve.
example.gloss: I really loved the dinner, we have to do it again soon.

example.target: A paragem do autocarro fica mesmo à frente do hotel.
example.gloss: The bus stop is right in front of the hotel.

example.target: Não te preocupes com esse teste, aquilo é canja.
example.gloss: Don't worry about that test, it's a piece of cake.

example.target: Custa a crer que ele tenha desistido mesmo antes do fim.
example.gloss: It's hard to believe that he gave up right before the end.

example.target: Vale a pena acordar cedo para ver o nascer do sol na serra.
example.gloss: It's worth waking up early to see the sunrise in the mountains.

words.en: to be possible, to allow for

example.target: Não dá para ouvir nada com este barulho todo.
example.gloss: It's impossible to hear anything with all this noise.

words.en: to have to deal with, to answer to (someone)
words.note: often as a warning: hás de te haver comigo

example.target: Se voltares a mexer nas minhas coisas, hás de te haver comigo.
example.gloss: If you touch my things again, you'll have me to deal with.

words.en: to get going; to clear off
words.note: informal; can sound brusque as an order

example.target: Quando percebeu que a conversa não ia dar em nada, pôs-se a andar.
example.gloss: When he realised the conversation was going nowhere, he took off.

words.note: very European construction: vou ter contigo

example.target: Vou ter contigo ao café assim que sair do trabalho.
example.gloss: I'll meet you at the café as soon as I leave work.

example.target: Dá uma vista de olhos ao relatório antes de o enviarmos.
example.gloss: Have a quick look at the report before we send it.

example.target: As críticas constantes deitaram-no completamente abaixo.
example.gloss: The constant criticism got him completely down.

example.target: Ninguém pôs em causa a honestidade da diretora.
example.gloss: Nobody called the director's honesty into question.

example.target: A câmara levou a cabo obras profundas no centro histórico.
example.gloss: The city council carried out major works in the historic centre.

example.target: É preciso ter em conta a opinião de todos os moradores.
example.gloss: It's necessary to take all the residents' opinions into account.

words.en: to notice; to cope with, to manage

example.target: Só dei conta do erro quando já era tarde demais.
example.gloss: I only noticed the mistake when it was already too late.

example.target: A meio da viagem, deu-se conta de que se tinha esquecido do passaporte.
example.gloss: Halfway through the trip, she realised she had forgotten her passport.

example.target: Fez de conta que não me viu e continuou a andar.
example.gloss: He pretended he didn't see me and kept walking.

words.en: by chance; as it happens, actually

example.target: Por acaso até gosto de trabalhar ao sábado de manhã.
example.gloss: As it happens, I actually like working on Saturday mornings.

example.target: Desculpa, não fiz de propósito, foi mesmo sem querer.
example.gloss: Sorry, I didn't do it on purpose, it really was an accident.

example.target: A propósito, já sabes quando abrem as inscrições para o curso?
example.gloss: By the way, do you know yet when registration for the course opens?

example.target: De vez em quando vamos jantar fora, mas normalmente cozinho eu.
example.gloss: From time to time we go out for dinner, but usually I cook.

words.en: at some point; for all we know (colloquial)

example.target: Às tantas ele nem aparece, já sabes como ele é.
example.gloss: For all we know he won't even show up, you know what he's like.

example.target: Daqui a pouco começa o telejornal, despacha-te.
example.gloss: The evening news starts in a little while, hurry up.

example.target: Falei com ela há bocado e estava tudo bem.
example.gloss: I spoke to her a little while ago and everything was fine.

words.note: in European usage 'logo' means later today

example.target: Logo à noite passo por tua casa para combinarmos tudo.
example.gloss: Later tonight I'll drop by your place so we can arrange everything.

example.target: Ontem à noite houve uma trovoada enorme que acordou toda a gente.
example.gloss: Last night there was a huge thunderstorm that woke everyone up.

example.target: Para a semana começam as aulas, aproveita os últimos dias de férias.
example.gloss: Classes start next week, make the most of the last days of holidays.

example.target: Hoje em dia quase ninguém paga em dinheiro, é tudo por multibanco.
example.gloss: Nowadays almost nobody pays in cash, it's all by bank card.

example.target: Antigamente havia elétricos em muitas cidades portuguesas.
example.gloss: In the old days there were trams in many Portuguese cities.

example.target: Atualmente trabalho a partir de casa três dias por semana.
example.gloss: I currently work from home three days a week.

words.note: informal; 'não ter piada nenhuma' = to be not funny at all

example.target: Essa história tem imensa piada, conta lá outra vez.
example.gloss: That story is really funny, go on, tell it again.

example.target: Que chatice, esqueci-me da carteira em casa outra vez.
example.gloss: What a pain, I left my wallet at home again.

example.target: O filme era tão chato que adormeci a meio.
example.gloss: The film was so boring that I fell asleep halfway through.

example.target: Fogo, já são cinco horas e ainda não acabei isto.
example.gloss: Damn, it's already five o'clock and I still haven't finished this.

words.note: very common in speech; also 'epá' at the start of a sentence

example.target: Epá, não sei, isso parece-me um bocado arriscado.
example.gloss: Man, I don't know, that seems a bit risky to me.

example.target: Muito obrigado pela ajuda. Ora essa, não tens nada que agradecer.
example.gloss: Thank you very much for the help. Don't mention it, there's nothing to thank me for.

words.en: no way, out of the question

example.target: Emprestar-te o carro outra vez? Nem pensar!
example.gloss: Lend you the car again? No way!

example.target: Ele está-se nas tintas para o que os outros dizem.
example.gloss: He couldn't care less what other people say.

words.note: idiom; literally 'to end up in codfish waters'

example.target: O projeto foi anunciado com grande pompa, mas ficou tudo em águas de bacalhau.
example.gloss: The project was announced with great fanfare, but it all came to nothing.

words.en: to be lost, to be floundering

example.target: Sem o manual, ando às aranhas para configurar a impressora.
example.gloss: Without the manual, I'm completely lost trying to set up the printer.

example.target: Consegui entrar no museu de borla porque era o primeiro domingo do mês.
example.gloss: I got into the museum for free because it was the first Sunday of the month.

words.en: loads of, a whole bunch of

example.target: Recebi uma data de mensagens enquanto estava na reunião.
example.gloss: I got loads of messages while I was in the meeting.

words.note: informal collective noun for a group of friends or colleagues

example.target: A malta do escritório vai jantar fora na sexta, apareces?
example.gloss: The office crowd is going out for dinner on Friday, will you come along?

example.target: Os miúdos passam o recreio a jogar à bola no pátio.
example.gloss: The kids spend break time playing football in the yard.

words.en: to have the nerve, the cheek

example.target: Ele ainda teve a lata de pedir dinheiro emprestado depois de tudo.
example.gloss: He even had the nerve to ask to borrow money after everything.

example.target: Não posso ir de férias este ano, estou completamente teso.
example.gloss: I can't go on holiday this year, I'm completely broke.

example.target: Mais vale prevenir do que remediar, leva o guarda-chuva.
example.gloss: Better safe than sorry, take the umbrella.

words.en: on top of that, to make matters worse

example.target: Perdi o comboio e, ainda por cima, começou a chover.
example.gloss: I missed the train and, on top of that, it started to rain.

words.en: come on; at least, thank goodness

example.target: Vá lá, ainda conseguimos lugar sentado no comboio.
example.gloss: Thank goodness, we still managed to get a seat on the train.

words.note: 'boleia' is the standard European term for a lift or ride

example.target: Dás-me uma boleia até à estação? Perdi o autocarro.
example.gloss: Can you give me a lift to the station? I missed the bus.

words.en: to put one's foot in it

example.target: Meti a pata na poça quando perguntei pelo antigo namorado dela.
example.gloss: I put my foot in it when I asked about her ex-boyfriend.

words.en: to do a lot of; to get fed up with
words.note: informal; fartámo-nos de rir = we laughed our heads off

example.target: Fartámo-nos de rir com as histórias do avô.
example.gloss: We laughed our heads off at grandad's stories.

words.en: to be rumoured; to appear (in a document)

example.target: Consta que a fábrica vai fechar no final do ano.
example.gloss: Word has it that the factory is going to close at the end of the year.

words.en: to take place; to elapse, to proceed

example.target: A conferência decorreu sem incidentes no centro de congressos.
example.gloss: The conference took place without incident at the congress centre.

words.en: to happen; to succeed, to follow

example.target: Sucedeu algo estranho durante a viagem que ainda não consigo explicar.
example.gloss: Something strange happened during the trip that I still can't explain.

example.target: O novo parque proporciona às famílias um espaço seguro de lazer.
example.gloss: The new park provides families with a safe leisure space.

example.target: O apoio da câmara viabilizou a construção do centro cultural.
example.gloss: The council's support made the construction of the cultural centre possible.

example.target: A falta de financiamento inviabilizou o projeto de requalificação.
example.gloss: The lack of funding made the regeneration project unviable.

words.en: to make an exception for, to caveat

example.target: O ministro ressalvou que os dados apresentados ainda são provisórios.
example.gloss: The minister pointed out the caveat that the figures presented are still provisional.

example.target: A professora frisou a importância de rever a matéria todos os dias.
example.gloss: The teacher stressed the importance of reviewing the material every day.

example.target: Gostaria de acrescentar um pormenor que me parece relevante.
example.gloss: I'd like to add a detail that seems relevant to me.

example.target: A nova lei abrange todos os trabalhadores do setor privado.
example.gloss: The new law covers all private-sector workers.

example.target: Esta afirmação carece de fundamento científico.
example.gloss: This claim lacks scientific grounding.

words.en: to confine oneself to, to stick to

example.target: O orador cingiu-se ao tema e não respondeu a perguntas.
example.gloss: The speaker stuck to the topic and did not answer questions.

example.target: O relatório debruça-se sobre as causas do abandono escolar.
example.gloss: The report examines the causes of school dropout.

words.en: to come across, to be faced with

example.target: Ao chegar a casa, deparei-me com a porta aberta.
example.gloss: When I got home, I found the door open.

example.target: Não podemos prescindir da tua experiência neste projeto.
example.gloss: We can't do without your experience on this project.

example.target: Os comerciantes receiam que as obras afastem os clientes.
example.gloss: Shopkeepers fear that the roadworks will drive customers away.

words.en: to aspire to, to long for

example.target: A equipa almeja alcançar a subida de divisão esta época.
example.gloss: The team aspires to win promotion this season.

example.target: Ainda não se vislumbra uma solução para o conflito.
example.gloss: No solution to the conflict is yet in sight.

words.en: to scrutinise, to pick apart in detail

example.target: Os jornalistas esmiuçaram as contas da empresa durante meses.
example.gloss: The journalists picked apart the company's accounts for months.

example.target: Frequentou um curso intensivo para aprimorar a técnica de desenho.
example.gloss: She took an intensive course to refine her drawing technique.

words.note: very common in formal European usage: colmatar lacunas/falhas

example.target: A formação serve para colmatar as lacunas dos novos funcionários.
example.gloss: The training serves to fill the new employees' knowledge gaps.

example.target: O município quer fomentar o comércio local com rendas mais baixas.
example.gloss: The municipality wants to foster local commerce with lower rents.

example.target: A escola promove atividades ao ar livre todas as sextas-feiras.
example.gloss: The school organises outdoor activities every Friday.

example.target: O turismo impulsionou a economia da região nos últimos anos.
example.gloss: Tourism has boosted the region's economy in recent years.

example.target: O nevoeiro dificultou as operações de resgate na serra.
example.gloss: The fog hampered the rescue operations in the mountains.

example.target: A nova aplicação facilita a marcação de consultas no centro de saúde.
example.gloss: The new app makes it easier to book appointments at the health centre.

example.target: A seca agravou-se com as temperaturas elevadas de julho.
example.gloss: The drought worsened with the high July temperatures.

example.target: As medidas anunciadas visam atenuar o impacto da inflação.
example.gloss: The announced measures aim to lessen the impact of inflation.

example.target: É urgente mitigar os efeitos das alterações climáticas.
example.gloss: It is urgent to mitigate the effects of climate change.

words.en: at our place, in our household

example.target: Cá em casa janta-se sempre por volta das oito.
example.gloss: At our place, dinner is always around eight.

example.target: Muitos engenheiros portugueses foram trabalhar lá fora durante a crise.
example.gloss: Many Portuguese engineers went to work abroad during the crisis.

words.en: to be grappling with, to be struggling with

example.target: Ando às voltas com a declaração de IRS desde ontem.
example.gloss: I've been grappling with my tax return since yesterday.

words.en: to be left empty-handed, to be let down

example.target: Prometeram-lhe o lugar, mas no fim ficou a ver navios.
example.gloss: They promised him the position, but in the end he was left empty-handed.

words.en: to be in a bad mood, to be grumpy

example.target: Nem lhe fales agora, ele está com os azeites.
example.gloss: Don't even talk to him now, he's in a foul mood.

words.id: meter o nariz onde não é chamado
words.hr: meter o nariz onde não é chamado
words.en: to stick one's nose into other people's business

example.target: A vizinha adora meter o nariz onde não é chamada.
example.gloss: The neighbour loves sticking her nose where it doesn't belong.

example.target: Podes dar-me uma mãozinha com as compras?
example.gloss: Can you give me a hand with the shopping?

example.target: Esteja à vontade, sirva-se do que quiser.
example.gloss: Make yourself at home, help yourself to whatever you like.

words.en: at the last minute, just in time

example.target: Chegámos à estação em cima da hora, o comboio já ia partir.
example.gloss: We got to the station at the last minute, the train was about to leave.

example.target: Li o contrato de fio a pavio antes de assinar.
example.gloss: I read the contract from beginning to end before signing.

example.target: A explicação dele era completamente sem pés nem cabeça.
example.gloss: His explanation made absolutely no sense.

words.en: with one's heart in one's mouth, anxiously

example.target: Esperei o resultado do exame com o coração nas mãos.
example.gloss: I waited for the exam result with my heart in my mouth.

example.target: O acordo salvaguarda os direitos dos trabalhadores mais antigos.
example.gloss: The agreement safeguards the rights of longer-serving workers.

example.target: O presidente assegurou que ninguém perderá o emprego.
example.gloss: The president assured that nobody will lose their job.

example.target: Não te posso garantir que chegue a tempo do jantar.
example.gloss: I can't guarantee you that I'll arrive in time for dinner.

example.target: Ao rever as contas, constatámos vários erros.
example.gloss: When reviewing the accounts, we noted several errors.

example.target: A polícia está a averiguar as causas do incêndio.
example.gloss: The police are investigating the causes of the fire.

words.en: to weigh up, to consider carefully

example.target: Estou a ponderar mudar-me para o Porto no próximo ano.
example.gloss: I'm considering moving to Porto next year.

words.en: to consider as a possibility, to weigh up

example.target: A empresa equaciona abrir uma filial em Braga.
example.gloss: The company is considering opening a branch in Braga.
